title: Customize number column to remove the commas and to choose the number of digits needed from the form fillers
excerpt: The number column works but we need to be able to customize the column recieve a specified number of digits to ensure that the respondents of the forms do not enter the wrong number on the form. For example, when the num…

posts.author: Adeline (Adeline Chiamaka)
posts.cookedHtml: The number column works but we need to be able to customize the column recieve a specified number of digits to ensure that the respondents of the forms do not enter the wrong number on the form. For example, when the number required is sjust 4 digits, we need to be able to set the form to recieve only 4 digits after which it will indicate to the respondent that the number is incorrect as it has exceeded the values required

posts.author: MichaelTomar (Michael Tomar)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yeah, I wish Monday’s number column had more options like digit limits and better formatting.

A workaround I’ve used: switch the column to Text for the form, then add a form validation rule or automation that checks if it’s exactly 4 digits. It’s not perfect, but at least you can reject anything too short or long before it’s saved. For hiding commas, text columns also don’t format numbers, so it stays clean.

posts.author: Imke (Imke)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would love to be able to change the comma’s and dots/ Because this is teh USA-way: $#,###.##
But in the Netherlands it is exactly the other way around: €#.###,##
I want to be able to change this ‘view’ globally so that it is showed on dashboards with widgets, and normal boards with normal columns and mirrored columns.
